selection_policy.general_ai: top 10 scored English AI news items after event deduplication and topic diversification
selection_policy.engineering_ai: top 5 scored engineering AI items covering simulation, CAD, CAE, SPDM, PLM, digital twin, physical AI, scientific ML, and industrial AI
selection_policy.research_radar: optional research items from research/community sources
selection_policy.ranking_note: Score estimates readership/engagement when direct read counts are unavailable. Selection prefers topic diversity so one hot area does not crowd out the whole digest.

top_10_general_ai.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
top_10_general_ai.text: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price As Anthropic forges a closer relationship with the state of California, the federal government has made an enemy out of the OpenAI rival.

top_10_general_ai.title: Pair Nova 2 Lite with Claude for cost-optimized document processing
top_10_general_ai.text: Pair Nova 2 Lite with Claude for cost-optimized document processing In this post, we show how pairing Amazon Nova 2 Lite with Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 4.6 delivers an efficient solution for digitizing scanned documents at scale. We built a two-model pipeline on Amazon Bedrock for digitizing scanned yearbook pages. Amazon Nova 2 Lite handles native multimodal extraction in a single call: detecting photos, extracting visible names with coordinates, and returning page-level metadata. Claude Sonnet 4.6 then performs spatial reasoning to match names to faces based on page layout.

top_10_general_ai.title: How to Govern Autonomous Agents in Enterprise AI Factories

top_10_general_ai.title: Build an agentic AI healthcare claims pipeline with Amazon Bedrock and AWS HealthLake
top_10_general_ai.text: Build an agentic AI healthcare claims pipeline with Amazon Bedrock and AWS HealthLake In this post, we show you how to build an automated claims processing pipeline using two key Amazon Bedrock capabilities: Amazon Bedrock Data Automation for intelligent document extraction from healthcare claim forms, and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore for hosting an AI agent that validates and transforms the extracted data into FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperable Resources) resources in AWS HealthLake. You will learn how to combine these services to create an end-to-end workflow that reduces manual processing while maintaining accuracy through automated validation checks.

top_10_general_ai.title: Multi-tenant LLM analytics with row-level security: How we built a secure agent on AWS
top_10_general_ai.text: Multi-tenant LLM analytics with row-level security: How we built a secure agent on AWS In this post, we show you how PAR built a production-ready multi-tenant LLM analytics system that enforces row-level security through a three-layer architecture: cryptographic request signing with AWS SigV4, semantic validation on Amazon Bedrock, and programmatic data isolation via Split-Plane SQL. We demonstrate how each layer operates independently to reduce the risk of cross-tenant data exposure, even when the LLM itself is compromised or manipulated.

top_5_engineering_ai.title: Knowledge Base Support on AWS and Azure
top_5_engineering_ai.text: Knowledge Base Support on AWS and Azure Connect and interact with all your data in one place. Rescale now connects engineering data stored in AWS S3 and Azure Blob Storage directly to the Rescale Assistant through vectorized search. Once connected, engineers can ask natural language questions and receive fast, contextually grounded answers drawn from their organization's own data, rather than relying only on general AI knowledge or manual file searches.

top_5_engineering_ai.title: AI master class 2026: takeaways on turning physical test data into competitive advantage
top_5_engineering_ai.text: AI master class 2026: takeaways on turning physical test data into competitive advantage From raw test data to AI-driven decisions in just three days—here’s what happened at the AI Master class in Leuven, where engineers got hands-on with AI. Over three intensive days, participants progressed from AI fundamentals to building fully integrated workflows. They learned how to prepare AI-ready data, automate validation and feature extraction, and train models for real engineering use cases, including road-noise prediction and manufacturing quality classification. By combining physical testing expertise with scalable AI workflows, the master class demonstrated how organizations can accelerate decision-making, improve model reliability, and unlock the full value of their test data—today and in the future.

top_100_news_candidates.title: Agentic Simulation Report Generation
top_100_news_candidates.text: Agentic Simulation Report Generation Presentation-ready reports in minutes vs. hours. Rescale’s Report Generator Agent eliminates the gap between raw results and structured reporting. Once a simulation study is complete, the agent takes the results data and automatically generates a report aligned to a customer's own reporting templates, including findings, relevant charts, and recommendations.

top_100_news_candidates.title: Debugging production agents with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Observability
top_100_news_candidates.text: Debugging production agents with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Observability In this post, you learn how to debug production agent failures using built-in observability capabilities. We walk through common failure patterns, show how to analyze agent behavior with traces and metrics, and provide structured workflows for resolving issues such as infinite loops and tool invocation failures. This is Part 1 of a two-part series. Part 2 covers performance optimization and memory management.
